semantic.role: Project metadata and build configuration for the ArchSpine architectural governance toolkit
responsibilities: Defines the package identity and entry points for CLI execution
Specifies Node.js runtime version constraints and module system
Controls which files are included in the published npm package
Coordinates build, test, lint, and validation scripts
Lists development dependencies that scaffold the development environment
invariants: Node.js version must be >=20.18.1 and <21 or >=22 – a strict engine boundary
The module type is set to ECMAScript modules (type: module), enforcing ESM import/export syntax
The main entry point is dist/cli/index.js; binary symlinks (spine, archspine) are generated from that script
Only the dist/ directory and approved documentation/licensing files are included in the published artifact

English.role: This is the package.json for ArchSpine – a command-line tool for architectural governance, semantic code analysis, and AI‑driven codebase understanding. It defines how the project is built, published, and executed, and serves as the single source of truth for the npm ecosystem integration.

parameter_definitions.name: The registry name 'archspine' under which the package is published; must be unique on npm.
parameter_definitions.version: Semantic version identifier (1.0.0) that drives release life‑cycle, dependency resolution, and changelog generation.
parameter_definitions.description: Brief summary of the tool's purpose: architectural governance and semantic layer for AI era.
parameter_definitions.main: Sets the module entry point; all require/import of the package resolves to dist/cli/index.js.
parameter_definitions.bin: Maps CLI commands 'spine' and 'archspine' to the same JavaScript file, giving users two invocation names.
parameter_definitions.type: Forces all .js files to be treated as ES modules; affects import/export syntax and top‑level await support.
parameter_definitions.engines: Defines the acceptable Node.js range to prevent installation on incompatible runtimes – critical for feature availability and security.
parameter_definitions.files: Whitelist of directories/files for npm publish; exclusion patterns block __mocks__, source maps, and other non‑essential assets to keep the package lean and secure.
parameter_definitions.scripts: Automation hooks for development: build, test in unit/e2e/schema modes, linting, formatting, and release gating. Each script invokes a custom Node script or vitest.
parameter_definitions.license: Apache-2.0 license identifier, determining legal usage rights and obligations.
parameter_definitions.devDependencies: Development‑only dependency declarations (e.g., TypeScript, ESLint, vitest) that are not included in the final package but are needed for building and testing.

English.stability_and_risks: The package.json file is foundational to the project's stability. The strict engine requirement prevents accidental deployment on unsupported Node.js versions, reducing runtime failures. The ESM module type (type: module) may cause breakage in projects using CommonJS unless a compatibility bridge is present. The files whitelist minimizes attack surface by excluding sensitive or unnecessary artifacts from the published package. The build script (node scripts/build.mjs) must produce a valid dist/ directory before any binary can be invoked; if the build fails, the entire CLI becomes unusable. DevDependencies are numerous and could introduce vulnerabilities if not kept up‑to‑date. The scripts section centralises all automation; any misconfiguration (e.g., missing script) could block CI/CD pipelines. Overall, this file directly controls the project's delivery pipeline and runtime contract, and should be reviewed with every release.
